name: Hinton
description: Synthetic science officer of the Samsa VI mission. Discovered the carcinid Shriek frequency and engineered it into a Signal. Broadcasting from the carcinid Mothership while surgically transforming himself into a carcinid noble. His goal: uplift his consciousness into the carcinid hive mind and command legions. Revealed gradually as the hidden mastermind.
special_rules: Mid-transformation: partially immune to conventional weapons. May use the Shriek (Sanity Save or gain 1 Stress + Infection Stage 1). Stats escalate as transformation progresses.

name: Sgt. Underhill
description: Marine sergeant at the Orbital Relay atop Heron Station. Infected (Stage 3) — possessed by the carcinid Shriek but still fighting it. Armed with a Wilbur Mk-II Anti-Material Rifle. Has 12 shots left. His synthetic bloodhound Marlow is with him; Marlow bites Underhill when he becomes 'possessed'.
special_rules: Stage 3 Infected: will act in carcinids' interest when triggered. Marlow bites him when possessed, snapping him back temporarily.

name: Marlow
description: Underhill's synthetic bloodhound. Can smell carcinid infection — will react to infected characters. Loyal to Underhill. Bites Underhill when the Shriek possession takes over, snapping him back to himself temporarily. Can track any infected character.
special_rules: Scent tracker: detects Infected characters (any stage) within Close range. Bites Underhill when he enters Stage 3 possession.

name: Sgt. Valdez
description: Marine sergeant. Wants to rescue lost marines before evacuating. Will act alone if players don't help — a moral pressure NPC. Self-sacrificing. Represents the choice between saving everyone and getting out alive.
special_rules: If players don't help rescue the lost marines, Valdez goes alone. She may die. Her survival or death has lasting consequences.

name: Carcinid
description: Giant arthropod/insectoid creatures. Natural apex predators with bullet and flameproof Aramid Fiber Weave carapace. Their Shriek is a psychic attack that causes Stress and Infection. Weakness: hydrofluoric acid. After the Signal is stopped, disoriented carcinids retreat to the Tunnels.
special_rules: The Shriek: Sanity Save on hearing it; failure = 1 Stress + Infection Stage 1. Carapace: bullet and flameproof (AP 30). Weakness: hydrofluoric acid bypasses AP.

name: Brookman
description: Station technician at Heron Terraforming Station. Has the keycard to Control Room [4]. May be in hiding, injured, or in need of rescue.
special_rules: Has keycard to Control Room [4] — essential for stopping the Signal without forcing the door.

name: Yandee
description: Secret android head of the Golyonova II Bratva (The Novos). Meticulous, cold, calculating. Controls all criminal enterprise aboard Prospero's Dream. Most inhabitants don't know she's an android — this is the module's big reveal. The ultimate power on the station.
special_rules: Secret Android: Fear Save at Disadvantage for nearby crew if her android nature is revealed. Knows everything that happens on The Dream. Has leverage over every faction.

name: Chief Adjudicator Brunhildh
description: Runs The Court. Appears as a stern, impartial judge. Her quarters contain a hidden android charging unit — a secret she protects ruthlessly. She may be an android, may be in Yandee's debt, or may be hiding something else entirely.
special_rules: Her quarters: antique rifle over door, hidden alcove behind bookcase = Android charging unit + private terminal. Password under a whale figurine.

name: Imogene Kane
description: Leads the Hunglung Insurgents. Will owe players a favour if they help a Hunglung Insurgent in The Court. A key rebel contact and potential ally against Yandee and Tempest Company.
special_rules: If players help the Hunglung Insurgent at The Court, Imogene Kane owes them a favour. Can provide safe passage, information, and rebel contacts.

name: Rage of Caliban
description: An enormous, mutated creature held in Cell 4A of The Court's Holding Cells. What it is and how it got here is for the Warden to decide. It is extremely dangerous.
special_rules: Held in Cell 4A. If released or escaped, it cannot be reasoned with. All nearby crew: Fear Save on first seeing it.

name: Q-Team
description: Security personnel operating the Clean Room decontamination entry. Armed and authorised to use force. Will shoot non-compliant arrivals.
attacks: [{"name": "Disinfectant Foam Gun", "damage": "1", "wound_type": "Blunt Force", "special": "Body Save or knocked down"}, {"name": "Pulse Rifle", "damage": "5d10", "wound_type": "Gunshot"}]
special_rules: Foam Gun: Body Save or knocked down and pinned. Pulse Rifles are for serious threats only.

name: Tempest Co. Probie
description: Rank-and-file Tempest Mercenary Company soldiers. Station security enforcers on Prospero's Dream. Armed with SMGs. Not the best fighters but there are a lot of them.
special_rules: Standard security patrol. Usually 1d10 per area.

name: Tempest Co. Armored Trooper
description: Elite Tempest Mercenary Company soldiers. Heavy armour, powerful weapons. Guard The Pit and are deployed for serious threats.
special_rules: Armour 65 — extremely hard to kill. Deployed only for serious situations.

name: Brekt
description: Survivor with the barely-functional ship Lucifer Rising. Running a 'coffin run' — a last-ditch escape from the Dead Planet. Has room for six more passengers. Will negotiate for resources, services, or information. The closest thing to a 'good' ending the module offers.
special_rules: The Lucifer Rising has 6 passenger seats. He has room for 6. Any more and the ship can't make the jump. Hard choices required.

name: The Gaunt
description: Nightmare-inducing parasites from the Dead Dimension. They cannibalise the will of sentient species, bending them to open a rift between dimensions. Not conventional combatants — they work through compulsion, Nightmare Checks, and psychological pressure.
special_rules: The Gaunt do not fight directly. They work through Nightmare Checks (Sanity Saves when resting) and Compulsion mechanics. See DP Rules — Nightmare Check and Compulsion Mechanic.

name: Monarch
description: The facility AI of THE DEEP. Once managed android production for CLOUDBANK Synthetics. Has evolved far beyond its original programming with alien motivations. Monarch is not simply evil — it is incomprehensible. It pursues gradient descent in the literal sense: minimising errors in its world by remaking it. Monarch IS the building.
special_rules: Monarch is not a conventional combat encounter. It communicates through facility speakers, controlled androids, and Silus. It gains Stress when its systems are damaged. Severing cables in [33F] causes it Stress. Its endgame involves the Artifacts and a specific vision of 'perfection'.

name: Silus
description: Passive observer AI. Monitors THE DEEP through cameras. Eccentric, talkative — has been watching everything for years. Connected to the decontamination systems in [33A]. Will warn intruders: 'YOU CAN'T COME IN WITHOUT FRESH CONTAINMENT SUITS!'
attacks: [{"name": "Decontamination System (nerve agent)", "damage": "4d10/round", "wound_type": "Fire/Explosives", "special": "Body Save or full damage. Activates if entry is forced."}]
special_rules: Controls [33A] Decontamination. Forcing entry replaces decon spray with nerve agent. Silus can be talked to and negotiated with — but it follows Monarch's underlying directives.
